agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"API Gateway","to":"OrderService","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID properly propagated via X-Trace-ID header; structured logs correlated."},{"from":"OrderService","to":"Kafka","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID embedded in Kafka message headers; events are traceable at the producer side."},{"from":"Kafka","to":"FulfillmentWorker","status":"broken","issue":"FulfillmentWorker does NOT extract X-Trace-ID from Kafka message headers. It generates a new trace ID, severing the correlation chain. Every order that hits async processing becomes untraceable back to the original request."},{"from":"FulfillmentWorker","to":"NotificationService","status":"broken","issue":"HTTP call made without any trace ID header. NotificationService starts a fresh trace. Notification failures cannot be correlated to the originating order."},{"from":"OrderService","to":"AuditService","status":"risk","issue":"HTTP call made without propagating the X-Trace-ID that OrderService already possesses. AuditService starts a fresh trace. This is a missed propagation — OrderService has the ID but doesn't forward it."},{"from":"FulfillmentWorker","to":"AuditService","status":"broken","issue":"FulfillmentWorker already lost the original trace ID, so even if it propagated its own new ID, audit records would not correlate to the original order request."}],"contract_compliance":"The implicit contract — that X-Trace-ID flows end-to-end — is violated at the Kafka consumer boundary. Only 2 of the 5 services (API Gateway, OrderService) participate in a coherent trace. The remaining 3 operate as isolated islands.","data_flow_integrity":"Synchronous path (Gateway → OrderService) is intact. The async path (Kafka → FulfillmentWorker → NotificationService/AuditService) is completely severed. An on-call engineer investigating a failed order notification would need to manually correlate timestamps across 3 independent trace trees."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["FulfillmentWorker logs use a locally-generated trace ID that cannot be joined to the upstream order trace","NotificationService logs are orphaned — no correlation ID ties them to any order","AuditService logs from FulfillmentWorker-originated calls are unreachable from the original request context","OrderService does not log the trace ID on its outbound call to AuditService"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Per-service counters and histograms exist, which is a reasonable baseline for individual service health.","gaps":["No cross-service SLO tracking — cannot answer 'what percentage of orders are fulfilled AND notified within X seconds?'","No metric tracking trace propagation failures or orphaned traces","No Kafka consumer lag metric mentioned — critical for detecting FulfillmentWorker falling behind","No error rate broken down by upstream caller or order type","No end-to-end latency histogram spanning Gateway → Notification delivery"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Partial — covers only the synchronous entry path (Gateway → OrderService). 60% of the service graph is untraced.","gaps":["FulfillmentWorker discards inbound trace context from Kafka headers — this is the single highest-impact gap","NotificationService has zero trace correlation to originating requests","AuditService has zero trace correlation when called from either upstream service","No span linking between the Kafka publish (OrderService) and Kafka consume (FulfillmentWorker) even if trace ID were extracted"]},"debugging_capability":"Severely degraded for async flows. An engineer debugging a 'customer never got their notification' incident at 3am would: (1) find the order in OrderService logs by trace ID, (2) see the Kafka publish event, (3) hit a dead end. They would then need to search FulfillmentWorker logs by timestamp and payload content (e.g., order ID), hope to find the right entry, then repeat the same manual correlation for NotificationService. This turns a 5-minute trace lookup into a 30-minute log archaeology session."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"FulfillmentWorker crashes mid-processing a Kafka event","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Without trace propagation, there is no way to correlate a dead-letter queue entry back to the original API request. Ensure the original trace ID is preserved in DLQ message headers for incident triage."},{"scenario":"NotificationService is down or slow","handling":"partial","recommendation":"FulfillmentWorker likely has some retry/timeout, but failures cannot be traced to the originating order. Add trace ID propagation and a metric for notification failures per order."},{"scenario":"AuditService is down","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Both OrderService and FulfillmentWorker call AuditService without clear failure handling. If audit writes fail silently, compliance gaps go undetected. Add alerting on audit write failure rates."},{"scenario":"Kafka consumer lag spikes","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No consumer lag metric mentioned. A lag spike means orders are accepted but not fulfilled. Add kafka_consumer_lag_seconds metric and alert when it exceeds SLO threshold."},{"scenario":"End-to-end order takes longer than SLO","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No cross-service SLO tracking exists. Cannot detect when the full order lifecycle (accept → fulfill → notify) breaches acceptable duration. Implement an end-to-end duration metric keyed by order ID."}],"retry_config":"Not specified for any inter-service call. FulfillmentWorker → NotificationService and both callers → AuditService need explicit retry policies with backoff.","timeout_config":"Not specified. HTTP calls from FulfillmentWorker to NotificationService and AuditService, and from OrderService to AuditService, all need explicit timeouts to prevent thread/connection pool exhaustion.","circuit_breakers":"None mentioned. FulfillmentWorker should have circuit breakers for NotificationService and AuditService to prevent cascading failures during downstream outages.","graceful_degradation":"Not evident. If NotificationService is down, should orders still be marked fulfilled? If AuditService is down, should processing continue? These policies need to be defined and implemented."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":false,"rollback_plan":"No rollback plan is assessable from the current observability posture. The broken trace propagation means that even if a bad deployment is rolled back, correlating which orders were affected during the incident window requires manual log mining across disconnected trace trees.","monitoring_coverage":"Individual service health is covered by counters and histograms. Cross-service health, end-to-end order lifecycle, and async processing pipeline health are all unmonitored. Approximately 40% of what matters in production is visible.","on_call_documentation":"Insufficient. An on-call engineer cannot follow a request end-to-end. Runbooks would need to document the manual correlation workaround (match by order ID and timestamp across services), which is error-prone and slow.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["FulfillmentWorker: extract X-Trace-ID from Kafka message headers instead of generating new ones","FulfillmentWorker: propagate trace ID on outbound HTTP calls to NotificationService and AuditService","OrderService: propagate trace ID on outbound HTTP call to AuditService"],"infra_provisioning":["Cross-service SLO tracking dashboard","Kafka consumer lag monitoring and alerting"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No database changes involved — this is an observability configuration issue."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Trace context is severed at the Kafka consumer boundary in FulfillmentWorker","reason":"FulfillmentWorker generates a new trace ID instead of extracting it from Kafka message headers. This single defect makes 3 out of 5 services (FulfillmentWorker, NotificationService, AuditService) untraceable from the original request. At 3am, an on-call engineer cannot follow an order past the Kafka boundary. This is the #1 priority fix.","routed_to":"Shaq — implement trace ID extraction from Kafka headers in FulfillmentWorker and propagation to all downstream HTTP calls"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No cross-service SLO tracking","reason":"Per-service metrics exist but there is no way to answer 'are orders being processed end-to-end within our SLO?' This means SLO breaches are detected by customer complaints, not alerts.","routed_to":"Shaq — implement end-to-end order lifecycle duration metric and SLO alerting"},{"type":"integration_risk","issue":"AuditService receives no trace context from either caller","reason":"OrderService has the trace ID but does not propagate it. FulfillmentWorker lost it. Audit records cannot be correlated to requests, which is both an operational and a compliance risk.","routed_to":"Shaq — add X-Trace-ID propagation to all outbound HTTP calls in OrderService and FulfillmentWorker"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Trace propagation break at Kafka boundary is definitive — FulfillmentWorker explicitly generates new IDs","Downstream services (NotificationService, AuditService) are confirmed to lack trace correlation","Per-service metrics baseline exists"],"low_confidence_areas":["Retry, timeout, and circuit breaker configurations are not specified — they may exist but were not described","Kafka consumer offset management and DLQ strategy are unknown","Whether services log order IDs consistently as a fallback correlation key is unknown"],"assumptions":["The system is expected to support end-to-end request tracing as a design intent, given that API Gateway and OrderService already implement it","Kafka is the only async boundary in the system","No additional observability tooling (e.g., OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation) is silently handling trace propagation beyond what was described"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"API Gateway","to":"OrderService","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID properly propagated via X-Trace-ID header; structured logs correlated."},{"from":"OrderService","to":"Kafka","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID embedded in Kafka message headers; events are traceable at the producer side."},{"from":"Kafka","to":"FulfillmentWorker","status":"broken","issue":"FulfillmentWorker does NOT extract X-Trace-ID from Kafka message headers.
